candidates.text: Another day another massive JavaScript supply chain attack. Replit users are safe and protected. https://t.co/AZZgQEh7uJ

Wrote about today's GitLab restructuring / "workforce reduction" announcement, and ended up digging around in version control for both the GitLab and the 37signals public employee handbooks to help illustrate my thoughts https://t.co/xkqehsa5hT

Supply chain attacks are happening left and right with npm, PyPI and so many other places. It seems to be getting worse, everyone agrees.

But what can you do about it?

Some thoughts on possible approaches (all have tradeoffs). 

What did I miss? And what vendors actually work?

OpenAI just admitted the model is no longer the hard part.

The OpenAI Deployment Company launches with $4B and a mandate to embed engineers directly inside client organizations.
Backed by Goldman, SoftBank, McKinsey, Bain, Capgemini.
